Summary: Auth & Access Control
Description: User/admin authentication and role-based access.

Summary: Repair Intake & Tracking
Description: Customer device check-in and repair reference tracking.

Summary: Job Status Workflow
Description: Admin/technician booking status lifecycle and pricing.

Summary: Parts & Inventory
Description: Spare parts stock and usage-per-repair tracking.

Summary: Customer Notifications
Description: Automated email/SMS alerts on status changes.

Summary: Billing & Payments
Description: Order checkout and payment gateway integration.

Summary: Product Catalog & Media
Description: Admin product management and Cloudinary image uploads.

Summary: Deployment & DevOps
Description: Hosting, environment config, and CI/deploy pipeline.

Summary: Add rate limiting to the login endpoint
Description: 10 attempts per IP per 15 minutes on POST /api/auth/login to block brute-force credential attacks.

Summary: Define a distinct technician role separate from admin
Description: User model currently only has 'user'/'admin' roles. Technicians share the full admin account today — needs a scoped role and permission model.

Summary: Auto-generate unique booking reference with collision retry
Description: bookingRef generated from timestamp+random on booking creation; createBooking now retries up to 3x on the rare unique-index collision.

Summary: Encrypt device unlock password at rest
Description: devicePassword was stored plaintext and returned unmasked by the API. Now AES-256-GCM encrypted, excluded from default query responses (select:false), with an explicit admin-only reveal endpoint.

Summary: Add device-password reveal control to admin booking detail UI
Description: Backend GET /api/admin/bookings/:id/device-password already exists; no frontend button/UI wired to it yet for technicians who need it to unlock the device.

Summary: Consolidate duplicate booking-status update logic
Description: bookingController and adminController each had their own status-update implementation that had drifted (inconsistent guards). Extracted to a shared applyStatusChange helper used by both.

Summary: Fix in-progress/in_progress status value mismatch
Description: Frontend dropdown/filter/badge used 'in-progress' (hyphen) while the backend enum uses 'in_progress' (underscore); also missing the 'waiting_for_parts' status entirely. Caused a 400 on every status update to that state.

Summary: Allow editing repair price after a booking is marked completed
Description: The closed-booking guard blocked ALL edits once a booking was completed/cancelled, including price corrections — but there was no other way to set the final cost. Guard now only blocks changing to a different status, not same-status cost/note edits.

Summary: Track assigned technician per booking
Description: technicianName field exists on the Booking model but there's no dedicated UI to assign/reassign a technician on a job.

Summary: Add parts-used tracking on bookings with inventory deduction
Description: New partsUsed subdocument on Booking; admin can deduct/undo a part against a booking from the bookings detail modal.

Summary: Fix inventory race condition on concurrent part deduction
Description: Read-then-save stock check allowed two simultaneous requests to both pass and over-deduct below zero. Replaced with an atomic findOneAndUpdate guarded by quantity >= requested.

Summary: Auto-restock inventory when a booking with used parts is cancelled
Description: Parts already deducted for a booking were never returned to stock if that booking was later cancelled, silently drifting inventory from the shelf. Cancel flow now restocks automatically.

Summary: Low-stock alert escalation
Description: isLowStock flag exists and surfaces in the admin list, but there's no proactive notification/escalation when a part crosses threshold.

Summary: Wire email notifications to booking status changes
Description: nodemailer is an installed dependency but not yet called anywhere — status updates don't currently notify the customer.

Summary: Select and integrate an SMS gateway
Description: No SMS provider integrated yet. Needs a vendor decision (e.g. Sparrow SMS / Aakash SMS for the Nepal market) before implementation.

Summary: Add notification delivery retry and failure logging
Description: Once email/SMS is wired, a status update must not silently succeed while the customer alert silently fails — needs retry + logging.

Summary: Implement eSewa payment callback verification
Description: Order model has an 'esewa' paymentMethod enum value but no server-side callback/signature verification is implemented — currently nothing prevents marking an order 'paid' without a verified transaction.

Summary: Implement Khalti payment callback verification
Description: Same gap as eSewa — schema-ready, integration not implemented.

Summary: Order checkout supports Cash on Delivery / eSewa / Khalti selection
Description: paymentMethod + paymentStatus lifecycle modeled on the Order schema.

Summary: Add Cloudinary image upload pipeline for products
Description: Admin can upload product images directly (multer + Cloudinary streaming upload) instead of pasting URLs manually; server-side MIME/size validation.

Summary: Split frontend and backend into separate deployments
Description: Frontend on Vercel, backend on Render — resolved persistent 404s from trying to force a Next.js App Router + Express monorepo through a single legacy vercel.json builds config.

Summary: Fix CORS allowlist blocking the production frontend origin
Description: CLIENT_URL env var mismatch caused the CORS origin check to reject the deployed frontend outright with no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header at all.

Summary: Fix NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL missing the /api suffix
Description: Frontend env var was set to the bare Render domain without the /api prefix, causing every API call to 404.

Summary: Guard app.listen() behind a Vercel environment check
Description: server.js unconditionally called app.listen() at module load, unnecessary and potentially problematic once bundled as a serverless function.

Summary: Switch Render start command from npm run dev to npm start
Description: Production is currently running under nodemon (a dev-only file watcher/restarter), which adds unnecessary overhead on a hosted service.

Summary: Audit production environment variables on Render
Description: Confirm MONGO_URI, JWT_SECRET, DEVICE_SECRET_KEY, Cloudinary credentials, and CLIENT_URL are all correctly set — these are gitignored locally so nothing carries over automatically.
